title: Afternoon restart
shortEncouragement: The day is not ruined. We only need one clean next hour.

steps.title: Reset the room
steps.detail: Open light, refill water, clear one small surface, and put your phone away.

steps.title: Pick one useful task
steps.detail: Choose the smallest task that would make the day feel less lost.

steps.title: Do a starter sprint
steps.detail: Work with low standards. The win is starting, not finishing everything.

title: Make the pile smaller
shortEncouragement: You do not need to solve your whole life. Just reduce the noise.

steps.title: Name the pressure
steps.detail: Write every open loop in short phrases. No sorting yet.

steps.title: Sort into now, later, ignore
steps.detail: Mark only three things as now. Everything else can wait.

steps.title: Close one loop
steps.detail: Send one message, tidy one item, or finish one tiny decision.

title: Study warm start
shortEncouragement: You are allowed to begin clumsily. Momentum is built after the first page.

steps.title: Prepare the runway
steps.detail: Open the exact material, clear distractions, and write the topic at the top of a page.

steps.title: Preview lightly
steps.detail: Scan headings and examples. Turn one heading into a question.

steps.title: Focus block
steps.detail: Answer one question, solve two problems, or summarize one section.

title: Professional rescue block
shortEncouragement: A messy workday can still end with one solid delivery.

steps.title: Triage inbox and tasks
steps.detail: Find deadlines, blockers, and anything that affects another person today.

steps.title: Send one stabilizing update
steps.detail: Tell someone what is done, what is next, or when they can expect a reply.

steps.title: Ship the smallest useful piece
steps.detail: Finish a draft, review, ticket, or decision that lowers tomorrow's load.

title: Visible-space reset
shortEncouragement: You are not cleaning everything. You are making the next action easier.

steps.title: Trash and dishes
steps.detail: Remove obvious trash, cups, plates, and wrappers.

steps.title: Sort into three piles
steps.detail: Make keep here, put away, and deal with later piles.

steps.title: Make one clear zone
steps.detail: Clear the desk, bed corner, or floor area you most need tonight.

title: Low-energy recovery
shortEncouragement: Today gets a gentler strategy. Protect the essentials.

steps.title: Stabilize your body
steps.detail: Drink water, eat something simple if needed, and get a little daylight.

steps.title: Lower the bar
steps.detail: Choose must-do, nice-to-do, and skip without guilt.

steps.title: Start gently
steps.detail: Do five minutes of the most important task, slowly.

title: Soft landing tonight
shortEncouragement: You can close the day without judging it.

steps.title: Close screens and lights
steps.detail: Dim the room, charge your phone away from bed, and set a calm cue.

steps.title: Tiny reset
steps.detail: Put away five items, prepare clothes, or clear your bedside area.

steps.title: Release tomorrow
steps.detail: Write tomorrow's first task and one thing you are allowed to leave unfinished.

title: Clear tomorrow map
shortEncouragement: A simple tomorrow is easier to enter.

steps.title: Choose the three
steps.detail: Pick up to three meaningful outcomes for tomorrow.

steps.title: Block the first hour
steps.detail: Decide what you will do first, where, and with which materials.

steps.title: Remove friction
steps.detail: Set out anything that makes the first task easier to begin.
